draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers structured, precise information layouts over narrative explanations
facade.label: Dense data table
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard with rows/columns, metrics-first, scannable numbers over prose
facade.rejectImplies: Prose-heavy interface, story-driven explanations, fewer numbers per screen

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer warm, approachable language over corporate formality
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace conversational copy, emoji, casual phrasing—make the tool feel like a collaborator, not a CEO
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into polished, authoritative, professional voice—users want clarity and credibility over warmth

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers structured, precise information layouts over narrative explanations
evidence.implication: Dashboard with rows/columns, metrics-first, scannable numbers over prose

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces need persistent tool access without modal overhead
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: Persistent left panel with collapsible states will become a core pattern
facade.rejectImplies: Modals or tab-based nav will dominate the interaction model instead

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer warm, ambient lighting over harsh bright screens
facade.label: Soft glowing interface
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward calming, low-contrast color palettes and gentle luminescence in the workspace design
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward bright, high-contrast, clinical aesthetic with crisp typography and defined boundaries

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers soft, earthy color palettes over high-contrast or vibrant schemes for an AI workspace
facade.label: Warm neutral tones
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into beige, tan, soft gray, and warm whites; avoid neon or cool blues; create calming visual foundation
facade.rejectImplies: User may want bolder contrast, cooler palettes, or more saturated colors; explore darker darks or brighter accents instead

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer interfaces where actions have immediate visual feedback with no perceptible delay
facade.axisTargeted: Latency and perceived responsiveness — does the interface feel snappy (instant) or fluid (animated with intentional delay)?
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness; may prefer rapid state changes, instant confirmations, minimal transition time
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer guided transitions, breathing room between actions, or doesn't prioritize perceived speed over smoothness

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers visual scanability and quick pattern recognition over text-heavy layouts when designing AI workspace interfaces
facade.axisTargeted: Visual hierarchy — icon-driven cards vs. text-first density
facade.label: Cards with icons
facade.acceptImplies: Build modular card-based systems with leading visuals; prioritize glanceable affordances
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor text labels and descriptive headers; dense information layers with minimal iconography

draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI-assisted tasks, projects, and conversations in one unified dashboard.
